purpose: Host/VM-aware scheduled-task manifest for the brain product. One entry per live maintenance task, naming its cadence, command, connectors, and RUNTIME (host-write vs vm-read+draft). Consumed by scripts/register_tasks.py (the s07 registrar) and by routines/task-registrar's per-client registration steps.

locked_counts.note: THE LOCK from persistence-budget.md (C-s02, ratified). Any manifest entry with os_scheduled=true on the host beyond 1, or any os_scheduled=true entry on the VM, is a budget breach -- amend persistence-budget.md first.

tasks.cadence: daily 07:00, host local time -- date-gated branches fire inside the same run (Mon=health, Tue=integrity, Sun=digest, 1st=graphify-documented-only); see docs/cutover/persistence-budget.md
tasks.command_notes: brain maintain is the umbrella CLI verb (src/brain/core.py BrainCore.maintain, src/brain/maintenance.py maintain_branches): it runs sync --publish + brief unconditionally, then runs health/integrity/digest for real on their date-gated weekday (graphify is documented-only -- the graph build stays separate tooling per task-disposition.md row 7). This single command IS the full date-gated heartbeat persistence-budget.md promises -- the registrar's host leg installs scripts/brain-brief.sh, which now calls this verb (see s07 evidence).
tasks.notes: Host-only by construction -- the VM has no scheduler and registers nothing (client-access-model.md). Every write/sign/maintenance verb is refused on role=vm before BrainCore is constructed.

tasks.cadence: daily branch of brain-nightly (zero extra OS entries); standalone form is on-invoke
tasks.notes: Index reconcile + drain drafts + status. _index.md regen stays vault-overlay (no brain equivalent).

tasks.cadence: Monday branch of brain-nightly; standalone form is on-invoke
tasks.notes: status + audit-chain verify + substrate self-test. Residual Obsidian/Bases-core plugin-hash check (if any client still runs Obsidian in parallel) stays a vault-overlay step, not a brain verb.

tasks.cadence: Tuesday branch of brain-nightly; standalone form is on-invoke
tasks.notes: Audit-chain verify + corpus-wide near-dup scan directly over the brain vector backend (e5-small ONNX) -- no MCP round-trip. Near-dup bands re-baselined for e5-small per execution-contract.md S5.

tasks.source_task: Acme-vault-curation (kb-curator: audit / lint-orphans / lint-stale / lint-callouts / propose-cleanup / refresh-index / rotate-logs)
tasks.command: brain curate --json (refresh-index + unclassified-notes lint); orphan/stale-link/contradiction/callout lint stays the .claude/skills/curation kernel skill (vault-graph structure, no brain-retrieval equivalent)
tasks.notes: Only the refresh-index sub-step folds to brain sync (inside brain curate). Run via the curation kernel skill for the graph-structure half.

tasks.cadence: on-invoke (Cowork, surfaced at bootstrap or fired manually -- never OS-scheduled, per the locked VM=0 budget)
tasks.command:
brain bases-query --where status=candidate --max-tier Internal --json   # find candidates (VM-allowed, no index-open) 
brain search "<topic>" --max-tier Internal --json                         # corroborate 
brain draft-capture --content "<proposal>"                                # stage an unsigned DRAFT, drained+signed by the next host `brain sync`
tasks.command_notes: NOTE: the host-only `brain promote-scan` CLI verb (added in C-s03/CUT-03) is a DIFFERENT, more powerful host-side triage ritual that opens the index -- it is refused on role=vm. The VM-eligible form of this task uses only bases-query/search/draft-capture, all on the VM_ALLOWED list (src/brain/cli.py).
connectors: Cowork chat session (manual paste-prompt; poke-only trigger, no cron)
tasks.notes: Promotion itself stays a human (P-10-equivalent) gate; this is triage/draft only. See routines/task-registrar's Cowork paste-prompt.

tasks.cadence: daily branch of brain-nightly (drain via sync); standalone/event-driven form is on-invoke on host
tasks.notes: Binary-extraction pipeline stays overlay tooling; only its re-index step folds to brain sync (the incremental indexer IS the drain).

tasks.cadence: monthly (1st), documented-only branch of brain-nightly; the actual graph build is on-invoke on host (separate tooling, no brain CLI verb yet)
tasks.command: (separate discovery-graph tooling -- not yet a brain CLI verb; relates to `brain graph-expand` as the consumer)
tasks.notes: task-disposition.md row 7: discovery-only graph, no clean fold into a retrieval verb. brain maintain documents the date-gate (branches_due includes "graphify" on the 1st) but does NOT invoke a build -- see src/brain/core.py BrainCore.maintain, the graphify branch returns invoked=false by design.

tasks.cadence: on-invoke (host or Cowork, on demand -- never OS-scheduled)
tasks.command:
brain search "<query>" --max-tier Internal --json
brain hybrid-search "<query>" --rerank --max-tier Internal --json
brain draft-capture --content "<research note>"
connectors: Cowork chat session (manual paste-prompt; poke-only trigger, no cron)
external research connectors stay human-initiated (egress posture)
tasks.notes: An analyst job, not a heartbeat. External egress (web search etc.) stays human-initiated regardless of client.

tasks.cadence: Sunday branch of brain-nightly for the scheduled emission (folded, host, zero extra OS entries); on-demand VM form is on-invoke
connectors: Cowork chat session (on-invoke VM form only; manual paste-prompt; poke-only trigger, no cron)
tasks.notes: `digest` is on the VM_ALLOWED list (read-only), so the on-demand form can run directly in Cowork without a paste-prompt registrar step if the owner just wants a one-off read. The registrar's Cowork prompt offers it as a named poke-only trigger purely for convenience (avoid retyping).

tasks.cadence: on-invoke (host) -- no SC/Bases/cascade coupling, near-pure pass-through
tasks.command: (vault-specific overlay tooling -- no brain CLI equivalent; brain has its own Ed25519 chain, this audits the vault's recommendation queue)
tasks.notes: Roster reconciliation: task-disposition.md names 10 tasks; the live-vault roster carries 3 near-pure pass-throughs not in that scope. All trivially OVERLAY-ONLY / HOST / ON-INVOKE, zero OS entries.

tasks.command: (vault-specific overlay tooling -- no brain CLI equivalent)

tasks.cadence: on-invoke (host) -- audits the vault's own audit chain + git coexistence, not the brain's
tasks.command: (vault-specific overlay tooling; brain's own chain is verified separately via `brain verify-audit` / `brain anchor` / `brain verify-anchor`)

totals.check: 13 manifest rows = 1 umbrella (brain-nightly) + 9 task-disposition.md tasks it folds/hosts/on-invokes + 3 roster-reconciliation pass-throughs. persistence accounting matches docs/cutover/persistence-budget.md exactly: 1 host, 0 VM.
